campaignCardDraftCopy: An empty sheet lays at the desk. Your mind swims with possibilities.
campaignCardInitFailedCopy: Something went wrong. Click to retry.
emptyHubRavenIntro: You sit at the desk, empty but for one paper. The sheet is blank but your mind conjures a large, black corvid gazing back at you. Its glowing, purple eyes lock with yours. “Master Wizard,” the raven whispers,
settingsSessionDescription: Sign out on this device. Your campaign data stays.
stepNameLede: What do you call this place? A working title is fine; you can rename it later.
stepNameTaglinePlaceholder: An autumn court, a debt come due, four ravens above the gate.
stepSystemLede: Search by name. Each system bundles a starter set of templates; you can adjust the bundle below. Or bring your own.
stepSystemNoMatch: No system answers to that name. Try the Bring your own card below.
stepSystemTemplatesLede: {systemName} ships with these. Toggle any off if you do not want them.
stepSystemTemplatesFootnote: Missing a template? Pick the closest fit; you can create freeform Things inside the campaign.
stepSystemByoDescription: A blank room. You bring the rules; we bring the wiki.
stepSystemByoNameLabel: What do you call your system?
stepSystemByoNamePlaceholder: ‘The Five-Card Oracle’ · ‘Homebrew d20’ · leave blank for ‘{byoDefault}’
stepPrivacyLede: Your familiar can be as quiet or as helpful as you want. We need an explicit choice on each of the two questions below. No defaults, no pre-ticked boxes. You can change either of these any time from settings.
stepPrivacyAudioLede: Some tables record their sessions and let the familiar transcribe; others keep everything in text. Tabletop sessions also trip up off-the-shelf speech models (fantasy names, system jargon, overlapping voices, different languages), so we tune our own transcription on real audio from people who opt in.
stepPrivacyAudioOptInTitle: Opt in. Record and help improve transcription.
stepPrivacyAudioOptInBullet1: You upload audio; your familiar transcribes and drafts recaps.
stepPrivacyAudioOptInBullet2: Used only to improve speech recognition models in your languages.
stepPrivacyAudioOptInBullet3: Never used to train generative AI or LLMs.
stepPrivacyAudioOptInBullet4: Never sold, licensed, or shared outside familiar.systems.
stepPrivacyAudioOptInBullet5: Switch off anytime; your audio leaves the training pool.
stepPrivacyAudioOptOutTitle: Opt out. Record, but don't train on me.
stepPrivacyAudioOptOutTagline: You still get full transcription. Your audio is processed, returned as text, and excluded from any future training run.
stepPrivacyAudioTextOnlyTagline: You'll paste notes by hand. Audio features stay off; nothing is uploaded or transcribed at any point.
stepPrivacyEvalsLede: Independent of audio, your familiar can send back anonymized signal about what worked and what didn't. Used to tune prompts and tooling, especially for less-common languages and systems where the defaults stumble.
stepPrivacyEvalsOnTitle: Evals are fine. Help us tune the AI.
stepPrivacyEvalsOnBullet1: Anonymized: no account name, no player names, no campaign title.
stepPrivacyEvalsOnBullet2: Your writing stays yours; never reused as content.
stepPrivacyEvalsOnBullet3: Used to improve prompts and tooling, not to train models.
stepPrivacyEvalsOnBullet4: Never sold, licensed, or shared outside familiar.systems.
stepPrivacyEvalsOffTitle: No evals. Keep it to yourself.
stepPrivacyEvalsOffTagline: Your familiar still works exactly the same. We just won't learn from what worked and what didn't in your campaign.
stepPrivacyFootnote: Either way, your data is never used to train generative models, and is never sold or shared.
stepReviewLede: Pressing the seal commits the campaign. You can rename, swap templates, and edit settings inside the campaign once it exists.
stepReviewErrorDetail: This is a known thin-slice failure: the platform minted your campaign id, but the initialization pipeline is not wired up yet. Your campaign exists in a draft state. Refresh the page and you can try again, or return to the hub.
wizardPayloadIncomplete: Wizard payload incomplete; please review each step.
wizardGenericError: Something went wrong; please try again.
